Description: Establishes the journey of faith and uses personal reflection to see God's hand in one's personal history.
Key Line: Not a checklist to finish, but a life that grows over time as you hear the word, accept it, and bear fruit.
Key Framework: Peaks and Valleys Exercise (your spiritual timeline)
Key Story: (none — introductory lesson)
Key Question: After finishing the Peaks and Valleys Exercise, what patterns do you see in how God has been working in your life?
Goal: Goal: You will understand what it means to begin following Jesus and take your first steps of faith.

Tools: Infographic: ABCs of the Gospel (Admit Believe Confess)
Description: Teaches the Roman Road (Admit, Believe, Confess), identifies baptism as the outward sign of an inward faith, and addresses the assurance of salvation.
Key Line: Salvation is not just a moment; it is the beginning of a whole new life.
Key Framework: ABCs of the Gospel: Admit, Believe, Confess (The Romans Road)
Key Story: The Greenhouse Metaphor: the plant that cannot make itself grow; God gives the growth (1 Cor. 3:6)
Key Question: Share about your salvation experience.
Goal: Goal: You will understand how salvation happens through faith in Jesus, gain assurance that you are saved, and learn what baptism means for your new life.

Tools: Lord's prayer and daily prayer app
Description: Provides a 6-step relational pattern for prayer using the Lord's Prayer: connecting with the Father, worshiping His name, praying His agenda, dependence, forgiveness, and spiritual warfare.
Key Line: Prayer is a conversation: talking with and listening to God.
Key Framework: 6 Steps of the Lord's Prayer: Connect Relationally, Worship His Name, Pray His Agenda, Depend on Him, Forgive and Be Forgiven, Engage in Spiritual Warfare
Key Story: (none — the Lord's Prayer itself is the framework)
Key Question: How are you growing your relationship with God by talking and listening to Him?
Goal: Goal: You will learn how to pray and begin developing a consistent prayer life.

Description: Defines Scripture as God-breathed and introduces the 3-question discovery method: what it says about God, what it says about man, and what you will do or believe.
Key Line: The difference between a life built on rock and a life built on sand is not what you know, but what you do with what you know.
Key Framework: 3 Discovery Questions: What does this tell me about God? What does this tell me about mankind? What is this passage telling me to do or believe?
Key Story: Nicodemus (John 3:1-4, 16-17) — a religious leader who comes at night and hears about the new birth
Key Question: What is this passage (John 3:1-4, 16-17) telling you to do or believe?
Goal: Goal: You will recognize the Bible's authority in your life and learn how to read and apply Scripture.

Description: Defines the church's purpose to love, equip, and serve based on the Acts 2:42-47 model of devoted fellowship and intentional community.
Key Line: You were not just starting a personal journey; you were joined to a family that stretches across the whole world.
Key Framework: Healthy Church Model: 1) Loves, 2) Equips, 3) Serves
Key Story: The Early Church (Acts 2:42-47): devoted to teaching, fellowship, breaking bread, and prayer; sharing all things in common
Key Question: How important is it for a Christian to gather with the church? If you attend, how often and why?
Goal: Goal: You will understand the purpose of the local church and your place within the body of Christ.

Description: Something amazing just happened in your life - and the most natural thing in the world is to tell someone about it. Introduces the heart of evangelism and a simplified version of the BLESS rhythm (Begin with Prayer, Listen, Eat, Serve, Story). A deeper dive into BLESS comes in 203.
Key Line: Evangelism is not a sales pitch or a program; it is one beggar telling another beggar where to find bread.
Key Framework: BLESS Rhythm: Begin with Prayer, Listen, Eat, Serve, Story
Key Story: (none — your personal testimony IS the story)
Key Question: List three people who can be a part of your personal ministry.
Goal: Goal: You will be encouraged to share what God has done in your life, learn the ABCs of the gospel, and get a first taste of the BLESS rhythm for living on mission.

Description: Frames repentance as an ongoing rhythm of the heart - turning from sin and back to God - that keeps your faith alive and your walk with Jesus moving forward.
Key Line: We do not repent to earn God's love. We repent because we have already received it.
Key Framework: Conviction vs. Condemnation; Starve the Flesh, Feed the Spirit
Key Story: Prodigal Son (Luke 15) — referenced: when we repent, God runs to us like the running father
Key Question: Was your guilt more like condemnation (driving you away from God) or conviction (drawing you toward Him)?
Goal: Goal: You will embrace repentance as a lifestyle and understand how it leads to freedom and deeper relationship with God.

Tools: Infographic: Attributes of God the Father
Description: Explores the Greatest Commandment - loving God with all your heart, soul, mind, and strength - while also revealing the character and nature of God as Father. Introduces the Trinity (Father, Son, Spirit) as the foundation for lessons 1-3.
Key Line: The Father's love doesn't lower the standard - it changes the heart that meets it.
Key Framework: Echo of Love - Receive / Respond / Remain
Key Story: Prodigal Son (Luke 15) - The Father runs first; the older brother mirrors the performance trap
Key Question: What changes tomorrow if God's face toward you is delight, not disappointment?
Goal: Goal: You will understand the Greatest Commandment, encounter the character of God as Father, and see how the Trinity - Father, Son, and Spirit - shapes the foundation of your faith.

Topic: Your Identity in Jesus the Son
Tools: Infographic: Who You Are in Christ identity statements
Description: You are made in the image of God and adopted through Jesus the Son - the second person of the Trinity. Roots your identity in the finished work of Christ, who restores the image that sin cracked and makes you a full child of the Father.
Key Line: You don't obey to be adopted - you obey because you are.
Key Framework: Identity Exchange - Guilty/Justified / Orphan/Adopted / Dead/Alive
Key Story: Jesus' Baptism (Matthew 3) - beloved before a single public work
Key Question: Where are you still trying to earn what Jesus already secured?
Goal: Goal: You will understand your identity as an image-bearer and adopted child of God through Jesus the Son, and learn to live from that settled identity rather than striving to earn it.
Key Bible Chapters: Genesis 1, Psalm 8, 1 John 3, Ephesians 1, Colossians 1, Philippians 2

Tools: Infographic: The Trinity - Father Son Spirit
Description: The Father loves you. The Son redeems you. The Spirit lives in you. Introduces the third person of the Trinity - who the Holy Spirit is, what He does in your daily life, and what it means to walk in His power rather than your own strength. This is the primary home for teaching on the person and work of the Spirit - anything about the Spirit that does not fit in 202.08 belongs here.
Key Line: The Spirit doesn't just help you try harder - He helps you trust deeper.
Key Framework: Keep in Step - Natural / Carnal / Spirit-Filled with Desire / Confess / Yield and Spiritual Breathing
Key Story: Peter - self-reliance to Spirit-boldness (Luke 22 to Acts 2) showing the difference the Spirit's control makes.
Key Question: What's one moment today where you'll practice 'Help me, Holy Spirit' before you react?
Goal: Goal: You will understand who the Holy Spirit is as the third person of the Trinity and learn to walk in His power and presence daily.

Description: Building sustainable rhythms of prayer, Scripture, fellowship, stewardship, and fighting sin as ways of staying connected to Jesus. Includes practical guidance on daily devotional habits - Bible reading, prayer, journaling, and Bible memory - as the daily rhythm that keeps the disciplines alive.
Key Line: Disciplines do not earn God's love; they position us to receive it.
Key Framework: Five Disciplines for Abiding - Prayer / Bible Study / Fellowship / Stewardship / Fighting Sin
Key Story: The Vine and the Branch (John 15) - Abiding is receiving; straining is not the same as bearing fruit.
Key Question: Is your current rhythm of life making you more like Jesus or more like your culture?
Goal: Goal: You will understand spiritual disciplines as means of abiding in Christ (not self-improvement), assess your current rhythm honestly, build a sustainable daily devotional habit, and commit to one or two concrete new practices.

Tools: Interactive Stepper: OT and NT Bookshelf walkthrough
Description: Welcome to a guided tour of the most important book you will ever read. The Bible isn't a single book. It is a library of 66 unique volumes written by 40 authors across 1,500 years. Learning how this life-changing book is organized will help you understand it and remember how the story of God fits together. First of three connected Bible lessons (map - road trip - skill).
Key Line: The Bible is not a single book. It is a library of 66 books written across thousands of years that tells one unified story.
Key Framework: The Bookshelf (Law / History / Poetry / Major Prophets / Minor Prophets / Gospels / Acts / Letters / Revelation)
Key Story: Josiah Finding the Book of the Law (2 Kings 22) - A king undone by hearing the Word of God for the first time.
Key Question: Has there been a time when a passage of Scripture hit you the way it hit Josiah and changed something inside you?
Goal: Goal: You will understand how the Bible is organized and feel confident navigating its books and translations.

Topic: How to Study the Bible - Discovery Bible Study
Tools: Interactive Stepper: OIA method (Observe / Interpret / Apply) with Psalm 23 practice passage
Description: Builds on the Three Discovery Questions from 101.04 with a deeper inductive method. Teaches OIA (Observe, Interpret, Apply) with a guided practice on Psalm 23. Third of three connected Bible lessons (map - road trip - skill).
Key Line: The Discovery Questions are your daily bread. OIA is your weekly feast. Use both.
Key Framework: OIA - Observe (What does the text say?) / Interpret (What does it mean?) / Apply (What does it mean for my life?)
Key Story: Philip and the Ethiopian Eunuch (Acts 8:30-31) - 'Do you understand what you are reading?' 'How can I unless someone guides me?'
Key Question: Which of the three steps (Observe / Interpret / Apply) do you tend to skip when you read the Bible?
Goal: Goal: You will develop confidence and skill in studying the Bible inductively and be equipped to feed yourself from Scripture.

Tools: Interactive Stepper: Four Acts (Creation / Fall / Redemption / Restoration) + BibleProject link
Description: Creation, Fall, Redemption, Restoration - the four-act story of the Bible with Jesus at the center. Second of three connected Bible lessons (map - road trip - skill).
Key Line: Every promise in the Old Testament is a shadow. Jesus is the sun that casts it.
Key Framework: The 4 Acts: Creation (God makes everything good) / Fall (sin breaks everything) / Redemption (God comes to rescue) / Restoration (God makes all things new)
Key Story: The Road to Emmaus (Luke 24:13-35) - Jesus walks two disciples through all of Scripture and their hearts burn within them.
Key Question: Which of the four acts (Creation / Fall / Redemption / Restoration) speaks to you most right now in your life?
Goal: Goal: You will trace God's redemptive story from creation through Scripture and see how Jesus is the climax of all history.
Key Bible Chapters: Genesis 1, Genesis 3, Luke 24, Revelation 21, John 3:16-17, Romans 3:23

Description: Four words explain what God did for you at the cross: Grace, Mercy, Justified, and Righteous. You will see how these four realities work together in a cycle that starts with God's love and flows through you to others.
Key Line: One tree broke the world. Another tree (the cross) healed it.
Key Framework: The Grace Cycle: Mercy / Justified / Righteous / Grace
Key Story: The Boy and the Fruit Tree - A son ruins his father's tree and his older brother pays the cost.
Key Question: How has the story of the boy or The Grace Cycle helped you understand what Jesus has done for you?
Goal: Goal: You will grasp the concepts of mercy / justification / righteousness / grace.

Topic: Living the Fruit of the Spirit
Tools: Infographic: Fruit of the Spirit (9 fruits visual)
Description: Sanctification is the process - the Fruit of the Spirit is the evidence. Explores how God progressively conforms you to the image of Christ through the Spirit's work, and how the nine fruits of Galatians 5 are the visible proof that the process is happening. Know who you are, then live out who you are.
Key Line: [IDEA] You cannot staple apples to a dead branch and call it an apple tree.
Key Framework: [IDEA] Root vs. Fruit (Works of the Flesh vs Fruit of the Spirit)
Key Story: [IDEA] The Vine and Branches (John 15) - A branch doesn't strain to produce grapes; it just abides.
Key Question: [IDEA] Which fruit of the Spirit is most obviously missing when you are stressed or tired?
Goal: Goal: You will understand sanctification as God's lifelong work in you and recognize the fruit of the Spirit as the evidence of that transformation - not a checklist to perform, but the natural result of walking with Christ.

Tools: Infographic: Two Sides of One Coin (faith + repentance)
Description: Our active response to the gospel: turning from sin and trusting in Christ.
Key Line: Repentance isn't just feeling bad about sin; it's walking in a new direction.
Key Framework: The Two Sides of One Coin - Faith (turning toward God) / Repentance (turning from sin) - inseparable in the same movement.
Key Story: Zacchaeus (Luke 19) - The immediate visible turn: repentance showed up that day in his wallet and his relationships.
Key Question: What is one specific area where God is asking you to change direction today - not just to feel differently but to walk differently?
Goal: Goal: You will understand that true faith and repentance are inseparable parts of responding to the gospel.

Topic: Loving Your Neighbors - Greatest Commandment Part 2
Tools: Infographic: Expanding Circles (home church neighborhood world)
Description: Explores the second great commandment - love your neighbor as yourself, love one another as I have loved you, the golden rule, and considering others better than yourself.
Key Line: [IDEA] Love is not an emotion you feel; it's a decision you make when it costs you something.
Key Framework: [IDEA] The 3 Rings of Proximity (Who is in your path? your pain? your calendar?)
Key Story: [IDEA] The Good Samaritan (Luke 10) - The religious people walked by; the enemy stopped.
Key Question: [IDEA] Who has God put in your regular path that you are currently treating like an interruption?
Goal: Goal: You will learn to express Christ's love practically toward others and see every relationship as an opportunity for gospel witness.

Topic: Loving Your Family at Home - Your First Neighbors
Description: Establishing a gospel-centered home where your family is your first mission field - and where forgiveness is most needed and most tested. Covers marriage, singleness, household life as discipleship, and the hard work of forgiving the people closest to you.
Key Line: [IDEA] The truest test of your faith is not how you sing on Sunday
Key Framework: but how you forgive on Tuesday.
Key Story: [IDEA] The Forgiveness Loop (Examine. Confess. Forgive. Rebuild) OR The 3 Chairs of Family Life
Key Question: [IDEA] Hosea and Gomer (Hosea 3) - Relentless pursuit of a broken relationship.
Goal: [IDEA] If the way you treat your family was someone's only picture of Jesus
Key Bible Chapters: what would they assume He is like?
Research Prompt: Goal: You will understand biblical principles for building a home that reflects Christ's love, embrace your family as your primary mission field, and learn to practice forgiveness where it matters most.

Tools: Infographic: One Anothers of the NT
Description: Living out the New Testament one another commands - serving in the church, caring for each other, resolving conflict, and what it looks like to truly belong to a spiritual family.
Key Line: [IDEA] A church is not a building you attend; it's a family you belong to.
Key Framework: [IDEA] Consumer vs. Contributor (The Restaurant vs. The Potluck)
Key Story: [IDEA] The Early Church (Acts 2) - They didn't just share space; they shared lives.
Key Question: [IDEA] If everyone at Neighborhood Church served and gave exactly like you do; what kind of church would we be?
Goal: Goal: You will understand what it means to belong to the body of Christ and move from attending church to actively serving and caring for others.

Topic: Discovering Your Gifts - APEST - Equip the saints for the work of ministry
Tools: Link: Free spiritual gifts assessment online
Description: Discovering your unique capacity for serving the body of Christ.
Key Line: [IDEA] God didn't give you spiritual gifts for your own self-fulfillment
Key Framework: but for the building up of His family.
Key Story: [IDEA] A.P.E.S.T. / Body Parts (Every part matters)
Key Question: [IDEA] Bezalel and Oholiab (Exodus 31) - Filled with the Spirit to build the tabernacle with their hands.
Goal: [IDEA] If you stopped serving entirely
Key Bible Chapters: what gap would be left in your church family?
Research Prompt: Goal: You will identify your spiritual gifts and commit to using them for the building up of God's kingdom.

Description: A biblical framework for when life gets hard - understanding that trials produce endurance, and that God is with us in the valley, not just on the mountaintop.
Key Line: God never wastes a tear; He uses our deepest pain to produce our greatest growth.
Key Framework: The Four Windows on Suffering - Result of brokenness / Used for growth / Context for God's power / Never the final chapter.
Key Story: Joseph (Genesis 37-50) - Sold by brothers
Key Question: falsely imprisoned
Goal: forgotten - and God meant it all for good.
Key Bible Chapters: Will you let your current trial make you bitter or make you better? What honest thing do you need to bring to God right now?
Research Prompt: Goal: You will develop a biblical framework for suffering and learn to trust God in seasons of pain and difficulty.
Key Photo: Psalm 22, Psalm 42, James 1, Romans 8, Genesis 50, 2 Corinthians 1, 2 Corinthians 12

Description: Worship is gladly reflecting back to God the radiance of His worth. This lesson explores what it means to worship God in spirit and truth - not just in a Sunday service but as the posture of your whole life.
Key Line: Worship is not a genre of music; it's a posture of the heart.
Key Framework: Gladly Reflecting Back - See God clearly / Feel your smallness / Receive grace / Respond in surrender
Key Story: Paul and Silas in Prison (Acts 16) - Praising God at midnight in chains - worship when it costs you.
Key Question: What are you practically magnifying with your time
Goal: treasure
Research Prompt: Goal: You will understand worship as the God-centered response of your whole life and learn to practice worship both personally and corporately.
Key Photo: John 4, Acts 16, Romans 12, Isaiah 6, Psalm 42, Psalm 95, Hebrews 10

Description: Breaking the power of money through tithing and stewardship as an act of worship and trust.
Key Line: Generosity is not primarily a financial discipline; it is a form of worship with your wallet.
Key Framework: The Generosity Ladder - Initial (starting somewhere) / Intentional (giving with a plan) / Irrational (giving that looks crazy from outside).
Key Story: The Widow's Mite (Mark 12) - Two copper coins and paradise-level trust: it's the proportion not the amount.
Key Question: Are your finances right now a reflection of your faith or your fear?
Goal: Goal: You will understand that generosity flows from gratitude and learn to give with joy and faith.
Key Bible Chapters: Psalm 24, Matthew 6, Mark 12, Luke 12, 2 Corinthians 9, Matthew 6

Description: Honoring God with our bodies and minds as a response to the grace we have received - practical strategies for fighting temptation.
Key Line: [IDEA] You don't fight lust by trying not to lust; you fight it by finding something more beautiful.
Key Framework: [IDEA] The Expulsive Power of a New Affection (Thomas Chalmers) OR The FIGHT acrostic
Key Story: [IDEA] David and Bathsheba (2 Samuel 11) - The danger of being idle in the wrong place.
Key Question: [IDEA] What 'harmless' compromise are you tolerating that is slowly numbing your affection for Jesus?
Goal: Goal: You will commit to holiness in thought, word, and deed as an expression of your love for Christ and learn practical strategies for fighting temptation.

Tools: Infographic: Two Ordinances side by side
Description: The meaning and beauty of Baptism and the Lord's Supper as symbols of the gospel - and how the Lord's Supper points forward to the return of Christ.
Key Line: [IDEA] Baptism is our wedding ring to the world; Communion is our anniversary dinner with Jesus.
Key Framework: [IDEA] The Two Ordinances (Sign of Entrance vs. Sign of Ongoing Fellowship)
Key Story: [IDEA] The Last Supper (Luke 22) - A meal anticipating the final kingdom banquet.
Key Question: [IDEA] How does participating in the Lord's Supper recalibrate your focus from your failures to His finished work?
Goal: Goal: You will understand the meaning and importance of baptism and the Lord's Supper and what they declare about the gospel and our future hope.

Topic: The Great Commission - Your Personal Ministry
Description: The last words of Jesus and what they mean for every believer: you are sent. Great Commission vision with Acts 1:8 concentric circles and the home as first mission field.
Key Line: seek
Key Framework: seek
Key Story: seek
Key Question: seek
Goal: Goal: You will understand Jesus' command to make disciples and your personal role in fulfilling it.

Tools: BLESS logo + potential BLESS Tracker app
Description: The deeper dive into the BLESS rhythm introduced in 101.06. Five practical rhythms for living on mission where you live, work, and play: Begin with Prayer, Listen, Eat, Serve, Story. This is the skill-training version of what 101 introduced as heart preparation.
Key Line: seek
Key Framework: seek
Key Story: seek
Key Question: seek
Goal: Goal: You will master the BLESS rhythms and begin applying them as a daily framework for everyday mission.

Tools: Infographic: The Open Table (hospitality IS vs ISN'T)
Description: Life Skill: Using your home and your table to welcome strangers into the family of God - why hospitality is not entertaining but mission.
Key Line: seek
Key Framework: seek
Key Story: seek
Key Question: seek
Goal: Goal: You will see hospitality as a spiritual practice and a powerful way to show Christ's love to others.

Topic: Sharing Your Story and Jesus' Story
Tools: Doc: How to Write Your Testimony + ministrybag.com/thegospeldiagram
Description: Life Skill: Crafting and sharing your personal testimony and the gospel diagram - the STORY letter of BLESS, and the bridge illustration.
Key Line: seek
Key Framework: seek
Key Story: seek
Key Question: seek
Goal: Goal: You will craft and practice sharing your personal testimony alongside the gospel clearly and reproducibly.

Topic: Faith at Work - Integrity Ambition and Calling
Tools: Infographic: Daniel at Work - integrity principles
Description: You already know how to live on mission where you work (BLESS). This lesson goes deeper into the real tensions believers face Monday through Friday - integrity under pressure, ambition vs. contentment, rest in a hustle culture, what to do when your boss asks you to compromise, and how to think about calling vs. career.
Key Line: [IDEA] Your job is not just how you make a living; it's where you make a difference.
Key Framework: [IDEA] The Daniel Model: Uncompromising Excellence + Unapologetic Faith
Key Story: [IDEA] Daniel in Babylon (Daniel 1 & 6) - Refusing the king's food but serving the king's city.
Key Question: [IDEA] Where are you currently tempted to compromise your integrity for the sake of your ambition?
Goal: Goal: You will learn to navigate the unique tensions of faith in the workplace and develop a biblical framework for integrity and calling in your career.

Tools: Infographic: Top 5 Hard Questions with starting points
Description: Helping believers answer why for the hope within them - engaging culture with grace and truth.
Key Line: seek
Key Framework: seek
Key Story: seek
Key Question: seek
Goal: Goal: You will learn to defend your faith thoughtfully and engage with skeptics and culture with grace and truth.

Description: Protecting the mission from the schemes of the enemy through prayer and truth and community.
Key Line: seek
Key Framework: seek
Key Story: seek
Key Question: seek
Goal: Goal: You will understand the reality of spiritual opposition and learn to stand firm in Christ's victory.

Description: The practice of life-on-life investment - how to intentionally walk alongside someone and help them grow in their faith.
Key Line: seek
Key Framework: seek
Key Story: seek
Key Question: seek
Goal: Goal: You will understand the DNA of discipleship and commit to investing your life in multiplying disciples.

Topic: Local and ParaChurch Ministries - Church Planting - International Missions
Key Line: seek
Key Framework: seek
Key Story: seek
Key Question: seek

Topic: Your Calling - Putting It All Together
Tools: Infographic: Your SHAPE (gifts heart abilities personality experience)
Description: Putting it all together: finding your specific team and role at Neighborhood Church.
Key Line: seek
Key Framework: seek
Key Story: seek
Key Question: seek
Goal: Goal: You will clarify your personal calling and commit to serving Christ through your church community.

Topic: Leaders Have Character, Integrity, and Accountability
Description: The foundation of leadership is who you are when no one is watching. Character, integrity, and accountability are not leadership techniques — they are the soil everything else grows from. Caught not taught.
Key Line: Your competency is capped by your level of character.
Key Framework: The Three Questions (Character: who are you when no one watches / Integrity: what do you do when you fail / Accountability: who has permission to speak into your life)
Key Story: Jethro and Moses (Exodus 18) — even the man who walked with God needed outside perspective
Key Question: Is the person your family sees the same person your church sees?
Goal: Goal: You will examine your own character honestly, understand why integrity is the foundation of all leadership, and commit to living an accountable life.

Topic: Leaders Have Followers: Let's Get Started
Description: You are not leading in the abstract — someone is actually following you. This lesson introduces the language of Your Personal Ministry and gives you a practical framework for walking your first (or next) disciple through the basics of faith.
Key Line: seek
Key Framework: seek
Key Story: seek
Key Question: seek
Goal: Goal: You will identify someone to invest in, understand the framework of personal ministry, and take your first step toward leading another person in their faith.

Description: Intercessory prayer is the engine underneath everything a leader does. This is not a repeat of learning how to pray — this is learning to cover your people by name, to pray before hard conversations, and to lead from your knees before you lead from your mouth.
Key Line: seek
Key Framework: seek
Key Story: seek
Key Question: seek
Goal: Goal: You will develop a practice of praying for the people you lead and understand intercessory prayer as the foundation of spiritual leadership.

Description: Great leaders find joy in seeing others connected to community — even community the leader is not part of. This lesson explores how leaders build environments of belonging and help people find their people.
Key Line: seek
Key Framework: seek
Key Story: seek
Key Question: seek
Goal: Goal: You will understand the leader's role in creating belonging and commit to helping others find their place in biblical community.

Description: The practical dynamics of leading a small group or Bible study — how to guide discussion, create space for the Spirit to work, handle the person who talks too much, and draw out the person who never talks.
Key Line: seek
Key Framework: seek
Key Story: seek
Key Question: seek
Goal: Goal: You will develop confidence and practical skills for leading a small group where people grow and the Spirit moves.

Description: How to prepare and deliver a simple teaching that helps real people meet God. This is not seminary homiletics — it is how to open the Scriptures with a group of 5 or 15 and make the Word come alive.
Key Line: seek
Key Framework: seek
Key Story: seek
Key Question: seek
Goal: Goal: You will learn a repeatable framework for preparing and delivering a talk that faithfully communicates God's Word.

Description: Servant leadership is not a chapter in a book — it is the posture of your whole life. Jesus led by washing feet and you will too. This lesson challenges the leader to make serving the default setting of their leadership.
Key Line: seek
Key Framework: seek
Key Story: seek
Key Question: seek
Goal: Goal: You will embrace servant leadership as a lifestyle and identify practical ways to lead by serving the people around you.

Description: By now you have learned to gather, lead, teach, and serve. Discipline is the unglamorous engine that keeps all of it from falling apart over time. This lesson addresses time management, consistency, follow-through, and the long obedience in the same direction.
Key Line: seek
Key Framework: seek
Key Story: seek
Key Question: seek
Goal: Goal: You will build personal disciplines that sustain your leadership and ministry over the long haul.

Topic: Leaders Lean Into Conflict for Peace
Description: The lesson no one wants but every leader needs. Correction in love, difficult people, maintaining unity without avoiding truth, and the courage to have the hard conversation that everyone else is avoiding.
Key Line: seek
Key Framework: seek
Key Story: seek
Key Question: seek
Goal: Goal: You will develop the courage and skill to navigate conflict biblically and pursue peace through honest, loving confrontation.

Topic: Leaders Challenge Others and Build Personal Ministry in Others
Description: The ultimate goal of leadership is to make yourself unnecessary. This lesson brings the series full circle — from your own character in lesson 1 to now building personal ministry into the people you have been leading. Spiritual multiplication, passing the baton, and sending others to do what has been done in them.
Key Line: seek
Key Framework: seek
Key Story: seek
Key Question: seek
Goal: Goal: You will commit to challenging others toward their own personal ministry and learn to reproduce leaders who reproduce leaders.
